item.title: Chair of India’s largest private bank quits over ‘ethical differences’
item.description: Central bank calms market saying ‘there are no material concerns’ about the health of HDFC Bank

item.title: Submit your questions to Katie Martin: are markets heading for a shock?
item.description: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with the FT’s markets columnist today at 1pm GMT

item.title: A test for chip testing and SoftBank’s stumble
item.description: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

item.title: Middle East war gatecrashes EU leaders’ economy summit
item.description: Also in this newsletter: DHL Express Europe looks on the bright side

item.title: Iran inflicts ‘extensive damage’ on site of world’s largest LNG facility in Qatar
item.description: Tehran vowed to retaliate against Gulf energy plants after its South Pars gasfield was hit

item.title: Kirkland & Ellis, the gilded age
item.description: The firm becomes first to break $10bn in annual revenues while equity partners take home $11.1mn

item.title: BrewDog’s new owner seeks female drinkers to revive brewer
item.description: Irwin Simon said he ‘would love’ to see more women drinkers and dismissed health concerns over moderate alcohol consumption

item.title: Spain is a role model in weathering Iranian oil shocks
item.description: The country’s speedy rollout of renewables has put a lid on electricity bills

item.title: Why bank cards may be heading the way of the cheque book
item.description: The £100 cap on contactless card spending is lifted today — but digital wallets matter more for future growth

item.title: Economists have caught the AI bug
item.description: It’s expanding the scope of research but challenging the profession’s process of weeding out mistakes

item.title: Calls for probe after ex-Army chief pressed minister to buy drones
item.description: Sir Patrick Sanders, an adviser to German start-up Stark, complained that the UK had no orders with the company

item.title: Trump sails into battle with Lloyd’s of London
item.description: The market’s historic strength in marine war insurance is being challenged by the US

item.title: Scale is not everything in asset management
item.description: Seeking size for its own sake can distract fund managers from focusing on clients and shareholders

item.title: US explored tying naval escorts in Strait of Hormuz to government insurance
item.description: Vessels seeking protection may be required to take out cover from programme run by Development Finance Corporation

item.title: When it comes to your pension, don’t believe the hype about private equity
item.description: Individual investors should be wary of the claims made — especially when they come from politicians

item.title: Tired of sports betting? Take a punt on babies
item.description: A new generation of gambling games can change the way we think about the structures governing our world

item.title: Apple supplier Murata starts US-China rare earths decoupling
item.description: Japanese manufacturer joins companies rushing to insulate themselves from geopolitical risk

item.title: European manufacturing misery? Some sectors have never had it so good
item.description: War, huh, yeah, whom is it good for?

item.title: The rise of deepfakes and how to stop them
item.description: FT AI correspondent Melissa Heikkilä steps inside the world of deepfakes

item.title: PwC US boss says partners who resist AI have no place at the firm
item.description: Consultancy begins overhaul of pricing and services in face of technology undercutting its business

item.title: Indonesia faces fuel fears as half its population travels for Eid
item.description: More than 140mn people expected to travel, putting pressure on energy demand as war in Middle East pushes oil above $100 a barrel

item.title: Japan rebels over $6bn fee for SoftBank under US trade deal
item.description: Dispute about power station payment shows Tokyo’s unease with governance of $550bn in investments

item.title: KPMG and Harvey Nichols among employers found to have paid less than UK minimum wage
item.description: Government steps up its enforcement of newly strengthened workers’ rights

item.title: AI is moving from answering questions to taking action
item.description: China is likely to be both the testing ground and a leader in the use of agents

item.title: UK to protect steel industry with new import tariffs
item.description: Move brings Britain in line with US, EU and Canada which have sought to bolster domestic producers

item.title: FirstFT: Pakistan and Afghanistan pause hostilities after Kabul attack
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Iran inflicts ‘extensive’ damage on Qatar LNG facility, and Fed chief says oil crisis will worsen US inflation

item.title: Russia considers sending armed naval patrols to protect ‘shadow fleet’
item.description: Putin ally Patrushev says Moscow could deploy ‘mobile firing groups’ after series of suspected Ukrainian attacks on ships

item.title: UK to procure more anti-drone missiles in show of support for Gulf allies
item.description: LMMs have proven effective in countering strikes by Iran and its proxies in the region

item.title: Unilever and Kraft Heinz held talks over food merger uniting ketchup and mayo
item.description: Exploration of deal worth tens of billions of dollars reflects struggle of both companies to combat subdued demand

item.title: Lex Greensill fails to persuade court to throw out UK director ban case
item.description: Australian financier must go to trial over fate after Insolvency Service launched probe
